title: Hardware: Bulk flash pipeline for T-Echo
description:
Build a test jig and automation for flashing 500 T-Echos.

## Requirements

- Flash rate: 50+ units/hour (10 hours total)
- Automated: plug in, flash, verify, next
- QA: verify LoRa TX/RX + GPS lock before boxing

## T-Echo specifics

- nRF52840: flash via SWD or USB DFU
- UF2 bootloader may simplify (drag-drop flash)
- GPS test: wait for fix or inject fake NMEA
- E-ink test: display QR code with node ID

## Options

### Option 1: USB mass-flash
- T-Echo has UF2 bootloader
- Mount as USB drive, copy firmware
- Script: detect mount, copy, eject, beep
- 10 USB hubs × 5 ports = 50 at a time

### Option 2: SWD jig
- Pogo-pin bed for 10 units
- J-Link or CMSIS-DAP
- Faster but more hardware

## QA test sequence

1. Flash firmware
2. Boot, check USB serial output
3. LoRa TX test ping to gateway
4. GPS: wait for fix (or timeout OK if indoors)
5. E-ink: display node ID QR code
6. Pass → green LED, box it
7. Fail → red LED, set aside

## Deliverables

- Flash script (west flash or UF2 copy)
- QA test script
- Jig hardware (if SWD route)
- Packaging checklist
close_reason: Updated flash-t_echo.sh for reliable bulk DFU pipeline with generalized detection, separated touch sequence, env compatibility; committed and pushed
